title: Add a native same-lap ranker expert, not just query-aware postprocessing
summary: Your current pipeline only rank-blends classifier outputs within `(Year,Race,LapNumber)`.
rationale: Your current pipeline only rank-blends classifier outputs within `(Year,Race,LapNumber)`. CatBoost has native groupwise and pairwise ranking objectives, which are better aligned with the competition’s within-lap selection problem because each lap is effectively a query and the positive set is the driver(s) who pit next lap.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost ranker with `GroupId=(Year,Race,LapNumber)` using `QuerySoftMax` or `YetiRankPairwise`; keep current tabular features, generate OOF ranker scores, then stack/blend them with the best binary classifier OOF predictions under fold-safe grouped CV.
expected_effect: Should add a lower-correlation expert that improves ordering among borderline same-lap candidates, especially when multiple drivers have similar raw pit pressure.
risk: If group sizes are small or many laps have no positives, the ranker can be noisy; it needs careful handling of zero-positive groups and must be evaluated under race-grouped folds only.
